name: Md. Tashin Parvez
headline: Product Analyst | Product Discovery, Requirement Analysis, User Workflows, Cross-Functional Delivery
summary: Product Analyst with hands-on experience in product discovery, requirement analysis, workflow design, and cross-functional coordination for digital products across SaaS, platform, and workflow-driven use cases. Worked with clients, designers, and engineers to translate business and user needs into scoped requirements, PRDs, user stories, and delivery-ready documentation. Strong foundation in user-centered problem solving, feature prioritization, and structured execution, backed by a Computer Science background and multiple award-winning product projects.

skills.label: Product
skills.value: Product Discovery, Requirement Gathering, Requirement Analysis, PRD Writing, User Stories, Workflow Design, Feature Prioritization, Competitor Analysis, Stakeholder Management, Cross-Functional Collaboration, User Feedback Analysis

skills.label: Analytics
skills.value: Data Analysis, KPI Tracking, Excel, Google Sheets, SQL

skills.label: Technical
skills.value: C, C++, Java, Python, SQL, MySQL, PHP, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Bootstrap

skills.label: Methods
skills.value: Agile, Scrum, User-Centered Design, MVP Scoping, Acceptance Criteria, Structured Problem Solving

education.degree: B.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering
education.detail: CGPA: 3.93 | Relevant coursework: Database Management Systems, Software Engineering, System Analysis and Design, Project Management, Artificial Intelligence, Data Mining

additional.label: Leadership
additional.value: UIU Computer Club — Programming Wing | Jr. Executive → Executive → Competitive Programming Wing Associate (2023 — Present)

experience.title: Product Analyst
bullets: Led discovery and requirement analysis across 20+ client engagements, translating business problems into structured scopes, user workflows, feature requirements, and implementation-ready documentation.
Worked with clients, engineers, and designers to define user flows, clarify requirements, and prioritize features based on business value, usability, and technical feasibility.
Reduced requirement ambiguity during pre-sales and early planning by documenting assumptions, edge cases, and workflow dependencies before development kickoff.
Supported solution planning and delivery readiness by aligning stakeholders on scope, open questions, and feature expectations across cross-functional teams.
Independently coordinated end-to-end planning and client handover for a production platform, managing requirement clarification, development alignment, and stakeholder communication.
Prepared PRDs, SRS documents, user stories, and workflow specifications to improve development clarity and reduce miscommunication during handoff and execution.

experience.title: Product Analyst Intern
bullets: Supported product discovery, competitor review, and requirement breakdown for client product initiatives across fintech, education, and operations-focused workflows.
Partnered with senior team members to map user journeys, organize feature requirements, and convert business needs into structured documentation for delivery teams.

experience.title: Undergraduate Teaching Assistant, Department of CSE
bullets: Supported instruction for ICS, SPL, OOP, and DSA-I courses, strengthening communication, structured problem solving, and technical mentoring skills.
Helped students break down complex programming concepts into understandable steps, improving clarity in technical communication.

experience.title: Grader
bullets: Supported course evaluation and academic assistance for Structured Programming, Object-Oriented Programming, Discrete Mathematics, and Electrical Circuits.

projects.title: Kairos
projects.subtitle: Personal Growth & Habit Management Platform
bullets: Designed a personal growth platform to address routine inconsistency and procrastination, defining core user journeys for habit tracking, journaling, progress visibility, and community engagement.
Prioritized MVP modules and structured backend/data requirements to support personalized goal management, user-generated content, and scalable admin operations.
Champion among 71 teams at the UIU CSE Project Show (2024).

projects.title: DirectEdge
projects.subtitle: Agricultural Supply Chain & Shop Management Platform
projects.tech: Workflow Design, Supply Chain Planning, Multi-stakeholder Platform
bullets: Designed a supply chain and shop management platform to improve visibility and coordination across farmers, agents, warehouses, distributors, and retailers.
Mapped multi-stakeholder workflows and prioritized modules for inventory visibility, warehouse operations, order flow, notifications, and analytics.
1st Runner-Up among 55 teams at the UIU CSE Project Show (2025).

projects.title: Wayout
projects.subtitle: Tour & Trip Management Platform
projects.tech: Travel Platform Design, Role-based Workflow Design
bullets: Conceptualized a travel planning platform to simplify destination discovery, package booking, guide hiring, and trip coordination in a unified flow.
Mapped role-based user journeys for travelers, guides, and administrators, prioritizing core modules for booking, reminders, navigation, and guide management.
2nd Runner-Up among 66 teams at the UIU CSE Project Show (2023).

projects.title: UIU Faculty Schedule App
projects.subtitle: Faculty Routine & Availability Search Tool
projects.tech: Campus Utility Tool, Search Workflow, Schedule Visualization
bullets: Built a campus utility to reduce friction in finding faculty availability, designing search and schedule-visualization workflows for faster academic planning.
Focused on usability and information accessibility across mobile and desktop experiences.

publications.title: AutoTrack: A Deep Learning Approach on a Novel Dataset for Vehicle Detection and Route Tracking from CCTV Images
publications.subtitle: IEEE TENSYMP, 2025
bullets: Developed an automated hit-and-run investigation system using YOLOv5 for vehicle detection and YOLOv8 for license plate recognition.
Reconstructed vehicle routes from CCTV feeds with 83% tracking accuracy.
